visa_types.name: Work
visa_types.documents_required:
Check lists and questionnaires:
Please choose the check list and questionnaire relevant for the type of residence permit you are applying for.
Please Click Here (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/) for check list
Note! Please be informed that in addition to the documents listed in the checklist, Chinese applicants also need to enclose a copy of hukou (all information pages) with English translation on a separate paper.
All Chinese certificates (birth certificates, marriage certificates, guardian relationship, permission to travel, death certificates, divorce certificates, etc.) should be legalized by the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs and the Norwegian Embassy.
Your necessary notarization and legalization(s) are required to submit original and one photocopy. 
All necessary Chinese documents need to be translated by an authorized translator to English, and it must be clearly stated who has performed the translation and when.
All the translated pages must be stamped by the translator.
All photocopies submitted should be in A4 format.
visa_types.processing_time: Please visit website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) or processing time: Click Here (http://www.udi.no/Norwegian-Directorate-of-Immigration/Oversiktsider/Case-processing-times/)
visa_types.forms:
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English only) and must be signed by the applicant himself/herself.
Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the visa application centre (free of charge). 
Declaration on Social Benefits

Information For The Public 

EXAMPLE Sponsorship form (individual)

To Download a Form: 
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
In order to be granted a residence permit as a skilled worker, you must be a qualified skilled worker whose expertise is relevant to the job you will be doing in Norway.
All applications for residence permits are handled by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI). You can find more information on https://www.udi.no/en/ (https://www.udi.no/en/)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
In order to be granted a residence permit as a skilled worker, you must be a qualified skilled worker whose expertise is relevant to the job you will be doing in Norway.
All applications for residence permits are handled by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI). You can find more information on https://www.udi.no/en/ (https://www.udi.no/en/)

## Visa Fee
Please Note:
Applicants who register their applications online using the UDI website (https://www.udi.no/en/) must pay the application fee with a credit/debit card (MasterCard or Visa card).

Please be informed that all the fees are non-refundable.
The visa fee must be paid when you submit your application.
Application fees applicable as of 01 July 2018:

Work:
Application for work permit: click UDI website for residence permit fee

## Logistic Fees
In addition to the Visa Fees, applicant requires to pay the VFS Service Charge as mentioned below:

- Hong Kong - VFS Service fee is EUR 38/ HK$347 per person (Non-refundable) with effective from 1st Jan 2024, inclusive of Service Taxes.
- Resubmission Courier Charge - EUR34.20 / HK$312 per person (inclusive of Service Taxes), if applicable.
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant pay 312 HKD to cover the Resubmission Courier.
Please note that all the logistic fees are payable in cash (HKD) or card at the time of the submission of the application.

## Documents Required
Check lists and questionnaires:
Please choose the check list and questionnaire relevant for the type of residence permit you are applying for.
Please Click Here (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/) for check list
Note! Please be informed that in addition to the documents listed in the checklist, Chinese applicants also need to enclose a copy of hukou (all information pages) with English translation on a separate paper.
All Chinese certificates (birth certificates, marriage certificates, guardian relationship, permission to travel, death certificates, divorce certificates, etc.) should be legalized by the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs and the Norwegian Embassy.
Your necessary notarization and legalization(s) are required to submit original and one photocopy. 
All necessary Chinese documents need to be translated by an authorized translator to English, and it must be clearly stated who has performed the translation and when.
All the translated pages must be stamped by the translator.
All photocopies submitted should be in A4 format.

## Photo Specifications
All photos must meet the requirements listed below. If these requirements have not been met, the  applicant will have to deliver a new photo that meets the requirements. The photo requirements are the same for children as for adults.
The Photo shall:
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on quality photo paper with high solution
- be in colours with a light background
- be 40-50 mm in width
- maximum 6 months old, resemble the applicant
- full-face, facing the camera directly, eyes looking straight at the camera
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face shall occupy 70-80 % of the picture
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair shall not cover the eyes
Regarding  glasses:
Glasses  are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible.
- the glasses cannot be coloured
- the rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- there cannot be reflection in the glasses
Regarding  head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks shall all be clearly visible
- Shadows in the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

## Processing Time
Please visit website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) or processing time: Click Here (http://www.udi.no/Norwegian-Directorate-of-Immigration/Oversiktsider/Case-processing-times/)

## Download Forms
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English only) and must be signed by the applicant himself/herself.
Please note that application form for Schengen visa should be signed twice. Application forms with one signature would not be accepted.
Visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the visa application centre (free of charge). 
Declaration on Social Benefits

Information For The Public 

EXAMPLE Sponsorship form (individual)

To Download a Form: 
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.name: Student
visa_types.documents_required:
Check lists and questionnaires:
Please choose the check list and questionnaire relevant for the type of residence permit you are applying for.
Please Click Here (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/) for check list
Note! Please be informed that in addition to the documents listed in the checklist, Chinese applicants also need to enclose a copy of hukou (all information pages) with English translation on a separate paper.
All Chinese certificates (birth certificates, marriage certificates, guardian relationship, permission to travel, death certificates, divorce certificates, etc.) should be legalized by the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs and the Norwegian Embassy.
Your necessary notarization and legalization(s) are required to submit original and one photocopy.
All necessary Chinese documents need to be translated by an authorized translator to English, and it must be clearly stated who has performed the translation and when.
All the translated pages must be stamped by the translator.
All photocopies submitted should be in A4 format.
You must bring your passport and all the documents listed in the checklist with you. You will find the relevant checklist when you register your application online. You can also find it on www.udi.no/en (https://www.udi.no/en) (checklists).
The Embassy will contact you if they need more information.
Applications will not be processed before we have received the passport. 
Applicants with passports that do not require a visa to travel to Schengen, will get their passport returned immediately after the Embassy has registered the application. 
If a visa is required, the Embassy will keep the passport until the application has been processed. 
Applicants may request that the passport be returned during the processing, but in such cases, the applicant must pay a second courier fee to VFS to return the passport to the Embassy in Beijing when a visa is to be issued.  
Applicants in need of a visa who know at the time of submission that they need the passport back sooner than the visa or residence permit processing time, should notify VFS staff about the date they need their passport back. 
Applicants who did not inform VFS about the early return of their passport, should send an e-mail to the visa section to get the passport returned before the processing is finalized.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For stays over 90 days, applicant must apply for a national visa according to the purpose of the stay.
All applications for residence permits are handled by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI). You can find more information on https://www.udi.no/en/ (https://www.udi.no/en/)
visa_types.full_text:

For stays over 90 days, applicant must apply for a national visa according to the purpose of the stay.
All applications for residence permits are handled by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI). You can find more information on https://www.udi.no/en/ (https://www.udi.no/en/)

## Visa Fee

The visa fee must be paid when you submit your application.
Application fees applicable as of 01 July 2018:

Study:
Application for study permit: click UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3592) website for residence permit fee

## Logistic Fees
In addition to the Visa Fees, applicant requires to pay the VFS Service Charge as mentioned below:

- Hong Kong - VFS Service fee is EUR 38/ HK$347 per person (Non-refundable) with effective from 1st Jan 2024, inclusive of Service Taxes.
- Resubmission Courier Charge - EUR34.20 / HK$312 per person (inclusive of Service Taxes), if applicable.
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant pay 312 HKD to cover the Resubmission Courier.
Please note that all the logistic fees are payable in cash (HKD) or card at the time of the submission of the application.

## Documents Required

Your necessary notarization and legalization(s) are required to submit original and one photocopy.
All necessary Chinese documents need to be translated by an authorized translator to English, and it must be clearly stated who has performed the translation and when.
All the translated pages must be stamped by the translator.
All photocopies submitted should be in A4 format.
You must bring your passport and all the documents listed in the checklist with you. You will find the relevant checklist when you register your application online. You can also find it on www.udi.no/en (https://www.udi.no/en) (checklists).
The Embassy will contact you if they need more information.

Applicants with passports that do not require a visa to travel to Schengen, will get their passport returned immediately after the Embassy has registered the application.

Applicants in need of a visa who know at the time of submission that they need the passport back sooner than the visa or residence permit processing time, should notify VFS staff about the date they need their passport back.

- be printed on quality photo paper with high solution
- be in colours with a light background
- be 40-50 mm in width
- maximum 6 months old, resemble the applicant
- full-face, facing the camera directly, eyes looking straight at the camera
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face shall occupy 70-80 % of the picture
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair shall not cover the eyes

Glasses  are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible.
- the glasses cannot be coloured
- the rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- there cannot be reflection in the glasses
Regarding  head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks shall all be clearly visible

Please visit website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) or processing time: Click Here (https://www.udi.no/en/waiting-time/)

## Download Forms
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English only) and must be signed by the applicant himself/herself.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit
visa_types.overview:
(Duration of stay more than 90 days)
For Schengen visas for Norway in Hong Kong, please visit our website:
If you wish to reside in Norway for an extended period of time (more than 90days), you need to apply for a long term visa (Student, Work Permit and Family reunification).
UDI will launch the new electronic form for D-visa from 13 January 2026, please find more information on Information from UDI.

All applications for residence permits are handled by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI). You can find more information on https://www.udi.no/en/ (https://www.udi.no/en/)
visa_types.full_text:
(Duration of stay more than 90 days)
For Schengen visas for Norway in Hong Kong, please visit our website:
If you wish to reside in Norway for an extended period of time (more than 90days), you need to apply for a long term visa (Student, Work Permit and Family reunification).
UDI will launch the new electronic form for D-visa from 13 January 2026, please find more information on Information from UDI.

All applications for residence permits are handled by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI). You can find more information on https://www.udi.no/en/ (https://www.udi.no/en/)

visa_types.documents_required:

Documents required:

- Cover letter (signed by the applicant) and receipt from the Application Portal.
- Check list and the relevant documents listed in the check list (only English or Norwegian)
- Questionnaire (only English or Norwegian) needs to be signed by applicant.

All the translated pages must be stamped by the translator.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you have a family member living in Norway, or you want to set up a family with a person living here, you can apply for a family immigration permit to come and live together with him or her. 
All applications for residence permits are handled by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI). You can find more information on https://www.udi.no/en/  (https://www.udi.no/en/)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you have a family member living in Norway, or you want to set up a family with a person living here, you can apply for a family immigration permit to come and live together with him or her. 
All applications for residence permits are handled by the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI). You can find more information on https://www.udi.no/en/  (https://www.udi.no/en/)

## Visa Fee
Please Note:
Applicants who register their applications online using the UDI website (https://www.udi.no/en/) must pay the application fee with a credit/debit card (MasterCard or Visa card).

Please be informed that all the fees are non-refundable.
The visa fee must be paid when you submit your application.
Application fees applicable as of 01 July 2018:

## Family Reunification:
Application for family reunification permit: click UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3593) website for resident permit fee
The following are exempt from this fee:
- Children below the age of 18 years
- Application from foreign citizens included in the regulations for residence permit in the EEA Agreement or the EFTA Convention, according to the Immigration Regulation §§174-189a, or renewal of such permit.
- Application for refuge or renewal of permit for foreign citizen that has been granted refuge, or is protected according to the Immigration Act §15, 1.
- There is no fee for processing appeals.

## Logistic Fees
In addition to the Visa Fees, applicant requires to pay the VFS Service Charge as mentioned below:

- Hong Kong - VFS Service fee is EUR 38/ HK$347 per person (Non-refundable) with effective from 1st Jan 2024, inclusive of Service Taxes.
- Resubmission Courier Charge - EUR34.20 / HK$312 per person (inclusive of Service Taxes), if applicable.
- The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant pay 312 HKD to cover the Resubmission Courier.
Please note that all the logistic fees are payable in cash (HKD) or card at the time of the submission of the application.

## Documents Required
Before you start to prepare your application please read carefully about family reunion here (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/).
Documents required:
- 2 recent passport photos
- Cover letter (signed by the applicant) and receipt from the Application Portal.
- Check list and the relevant documents listed in the check list (only English or Norwegian)
- Questionnaire (only English or Norwegian) needs to be signed by applicant.
Check  lists and questionnaires:
Please choose the check list and questionnaire relevant for the type of residence permit you are applying for.
Please Click Here (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/) for check list
Note! Please be informed that in addition to the documents listed in the checklist, Chinese applicants also need  to enclose a copy of hukou (all information pages) with English translation on a separate paper.
All Chinese certificates (birth certificates, marriage certificates, guardian relationship, permission to  travel, death certificates, divorce certificates, etc.) should be legalized by the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs and the Norwegian Embassy.
Your necessary notarization and legalization(s) are required to submit original and one photocopy. 
All necessary Chinese documents need to be translated by an authorized translator to English, and it must be clearly stated who has performed the translation and when. 
All the translated pages must be stamped by the translator.
All photocopies submitted should be in A4 format.

## Photo Specifications
All photos must meet the requirements listed below. If these requirements have not been met, the  applicant will have to deliver a new photo that meets the requirements. The photo requirements are the same for children as for adults.
The Photo shall:
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on quality photo paper with high solution
- be in colours with a light background
- be 40-50 mm in width
- maximum 6 months old, resemble the applicant
- full-face, facing the camera directly, eyes looking straight at the camera
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face shall occupy 70-80 % of the picture
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair shall not cover the eyes
Regarding  glasses:
Glasses  are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible.
- the glasses cannot be coloured
- the rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- there cannot be reflection in the glasses
Regarding  head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- Chin, forehead and both cheeks shall all be clearly visible
- Shadows in the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

## Processing Time
Please visit website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) or processing time: Click Here (http://www.udi.no/Norwegian-Directorate-of-Immigration/Oversiktsider/Case-processing-times/)

- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
